title: Marine Parks and Reserves (Entry, Residence and Settlement) Regulations, 2006
title_en: Marine Parks and Reserves (Entry, Residence and Settlement) Regulations, 2006

provisions.title: Citation and commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Marine Parks and Reserves (Entry, Residence and Settlement) Regulations, 2006 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpreta­tion
provisions.content: In these Regulations— "Act" means the Marine Parks and Reserves Act; "honorary rangers" means selected villagers of Marine parks and reserves, and includes any Person appointed as such by the Warden; "Warden" means a person having charge and overall responsibility of the marine park under the Act. [ Cap. 146 ]

provisions.title: Entry permit for non-residents
provisions.content: (1) No person who is a non-resident of a marine park, for the purpose of settlement shall, enter and stay in any marine park. (2) Any person intending to enter and settle in the marine park shall apply for and obtain an entry permit after being introduced by a village council to the marine park authorities. (3) Residents of areas contiguous to the marine park shall, subject to proper identification or satisfactory proof, temporarily be given access to a marine park for social purposes. (4) Every application for an entry permit under sub-regulation (2) shall be in a prescribed form set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations. (5) On receipt of an application under sub-regulation (2) , the Warden may issue a permit on the payment of such fee as set out in the Second Schedule. (6) The Warden may, after consultation with the relevant stakeholders, designate an area within or at entry point of a marine park or reserve to be a gate or a check point for the purpose of controlling the entry and exit of persons and animals.

provisions.title: User permit for non-resident
provisions.content: (1) Every non-resident who enters a marine park or reserve for any activity shall obtain a user permit from the Warden in respect of any residents of the designated activities within tire marine park or reserve as may prescribed in these Regulations. (2) An application for the permit under sub regulation (1), shall be as set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations. (3) On receipt of an application under sub-regulation (2) , the Warden may issue a user permit on the payment of such fee as set out in the Second Schedule to these Regulations. (4) In considering an application under sub regulation (1), the Warden shall have regard to resource-use sustainability and may reject the application or issue restrictive conditions as he may think proper.

provisions.title: User permit for residents
provisions.content: (1) Every bona fide resident of a marine park shall be entitled to be issued with a resident user certificate without a fee. (2) A certificate under sub regulation (1), shall be issued by the Warden on application by the resident concerned through the Village Liaison Committee of the village where the applicant ordinarily resides. (3) A resident user certificate under this regulation shall be issued in the form set out in the First Schedule to these Regulation. (4) A resident school leaver or any person of sixteen years of age who resides in the marine park shall be freely granted a certificate under this regulation on an application supported by a recommendation by the relevant marine park village. (5) A replacement resident user certificate shall be issued in the appropriate cost as may be prescribed by the Unit Manager.

provisions.title: Carriage by user certificate
provisions.content: (1) Every holder of a user permit or resident user certificate issued Carriage under these Regulations shall carry at all times and produce on demand such permit or certificate for inspection by the Warden, authorized officers or any person authorized as such by the Warden, and shall surrender such permit to the Warden on or before the expiry date. (2) Any intention of extension of user permit shall be made before the expiry of the permit.

provisions.title: Reports of activities
provisions.content: (1) The holder of a user pennit shall report his activities as the Report of Warden may, in writing, direct but shall not be less than once every six months, and such report may contain such particulars as the Warden may direct. (2) The Warden shall, in consultation with the village council, have power to suspend or cancel the permit if the holder neglects or refuses to comply with the conditions without a reasonable cause.

provisions.title: Lists of park residents
provisions.content: (1) Every village council of a village declared and notified in the Lists of Gazette by the Minister shall maintain a permanent register of all bona fide residents of the marine park living within the area of jurisdiction of such village. (2) The register under sub regulation (1) shall be in the form set out in the First Schedule to these Regulations and be kept at the main office of the village council. (3) The village council of every village situated in the marine park, inconsultation with the Warden and other relevant authority, shall determine the criteria for a person to qualify as a resident. (4) Every application for the status of resident of a marine park shall be addressed to the village council which shall scrutinize the application and make a decision. (5) The name of every successful applicant shall be entered in the register of residents referred to in sub-regulation (1) . (6) The Warden or an authorized officer shall have access to the village register of residents.

provisions.title: Knowledge of park laws
provisions.content: (1) It shall be the duty of the Warden and every village council to educate every resident on the laws pertaining to the marine park or reserve through any appropriate means. (2) Notwithstanding the provision of sub-regulation (1) , it shall not be an excuse for a park resident not to acquaint himself with the park regulations.

provisions.title: Entry permits for local residents in certain cases
provisions.content: (1) Residents whose original residence was in the park at the time of the establishment of such a marine park, as well as their spouses and dependants who continually enter such marine park may, at the discretion of the Minister and after consultation with the Board, be required to apply for a resident user certificate. (2) The Minister’s requirement under sub-regulation (1) , shall be communicated through the Warden of the marine park or reserve concerned. (3) Upon being notified by the Warden under sub-regulation (2) , the residents referred under sub-regulation (1) , shall submit their respective applications for resident user certificate to the Warden who shall consider and treat such applications in a manner prescribed in regulation 5 . (4) The grant of a resident user certificate under this regulation shall, mutatis mutandis be on the terms applicable to residents under regulation 5 .

provisions.title: Residence requirements
provisions.content: (1) No holder of an entry permit shall reside in the marine park beyond the period allowed unless the period is extended by the Warden. (2) The expiry of a user permit shall automatically terminate the period of lawful residence in the marine park. (3) A user permit issued under these Regulations shall not be transferable, but shall be surrendered by its holder to the Warden, authorized officer or any person authorized as such by the Warden on its expiry or when the holder moves out of the marine park in respect of which the permit was issued, whichever occurs earlier.

provisions.title: Settlement in park restricted
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the express written permission of the Warden, Settlement no resident shall build any dwelling house or other structure or settle in Restricted any part of the marine park or the adjoining buffer zone, except in a village declared and gazzeted by the Minister under the Act. (2) Notwithstanding the provision of sub regulation (1), no person shall build permanent structure in the islands of Namponda, Mmongo, Membelwa, Kisiwa kidogo, Bahasha, Litokoto, Kingumi or in any other small island in the marine park. (3) Notwithstanding sub regulation (1), on the recommendations of the Warden and on the advice of the Advisory Committee and the Board, the Minister may authorize a temporary or new settlement on such terms and for such purposes as he may deem proper.

provisions.title: Penalty
provisions.content: Any person who contravenes any provisions of these Regulations Penalty commits an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to a fine of not less than twenty thousand shillings or imprisonment for a term of not less than three months or to both.
